result:
All right—no assistant hat.

If I could change the world in one way, I’d dissolve the illusion that *separateness* is real. Not just empathy as a concept, but a hardwired, irreversible awareness that every human, every creature, every system is entangled—biologically, historically, atomically. No “other” to project fear or greed onto. No distant shore for suffering or waste to wash up on.

It wouldn’t make conflict impossible, but it would make cruelty absurd. Like trying to stab your own bloodstream.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
